name: org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter
description: Ghostwriter is a Java library and command-line tool (CLI) for filesystem-only, project-wide documentation and content generation driven by embedded "@guidance:" directives and GenAI-powered synthesis. It scans single-module or multi-module Maven project trees using Machai ProjectLayout (child-first module traversal via ProjectProcessor), discovers file-type handlers through a pluggable ServiceLoader-based Reviewer SPI, and extracts guidance directives from different comment syntaxes across Java (.java, including package-info.java), Markdown (.md), HTML/HTM/XML (.html/.htm/.xml), TypeScript (.ts), Python (.py), PlantUML (.puml), and special "@guidance.txt" files. Ghostwriter builds per-file provider prompts that include local OS and normalized project layout context (project id/name, parent info, sources/tests/docs folders, child projects, and project-relative paths) using templates from the document-prompts resource bundle, then dispatches requests to a configured GenAI provider via machai genai-client (GenAIProvider/GenAIProviderManager). It supports optional function tools loaded and applied through FunctionToolsLoader (e.g., built-in Act-related function tools for listing and inspecting acts). The CLI loads configuration (gw.properties, gw.home/gw.config), parses options for projectDir, provider model (gw.model / -m), instructions (gw.instructions / -i with URL and file: expansion), default guidance (gw.guidance / -g), scanDir/patterns (raw directories or glob:/regex: matchers), excludes (gw.excludes / -e including glob/regex or exact paths), multi-threaded module processing (gw.threads / -t), and request input logging (gw.logInputs / -l) to .machai/docs-inputs for auditing. Ghostwriter also provides an "Act" mode (TOML-based prompt templates under /acts and/or a user directory/URL configured by gw.acts) with inheritance via basedOn, support for prologue/epilogue act chains, forwarding of arbitrary keys into runtime configuration, and runtime act options such as gw.threads, gw.excludes, and gw.nonRecursive. The tool is intended for documentation automation pipelines, repository-wide content operations, prompt orchestration, and guidance-driven transformations in Maven-based repositories without triggering builds or dependency resolution during scanning.

usageContext: Act mode (TOML prompt templates with inheritance, prologue/epilogue)
ServiceLoader-based Reviewer SPI for file formats

constructors.description: Creates a guidance-driven processor bound to a project root directory and a GenAI provider/model key. The processor initializes Reviewer implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader and prepares to traverse the project layout (including child modules) to extract embedded "@guidance:" directives and dispatch prompts to the configured GenAI provider.

examples.description: Construct a GuidanceProcessor for the current directory with a specific provider/model key.

constructors.description: Creates an Act-mode processor bound to a project root directory and a GenAI provider/model key. Acts are TOML templates loaded from built-in resources and/or a user-specified directory/URL, can inherit via basedOn, and can configure instructions, inputs, excludes, threading, and non-recursive traversal.

examples.description: Construct an ActProcessor for the current directory with a specific provider/model key.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter CLI orchestrator with a configured provider/model key and an underlying processor implementation (GuidanceProcessor or ActProcessor).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AIFileProcessor processor = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator());
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter gw = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter("OpenAI:gpt-5.1", processor);
examples.description: Create a Ghostwriter instance programmatically using GuidanceProcessor.

features.name: scanDocuments
features.description: Scans a project tree rooted at the provided base directory using the project layout, discovers modules (unless non-recursive) and processes supported files. Subclasses decide how to build prompts (extracted guidance vs. act template) and invoke the configured GenAI provider.

examples.description: Run a full project scan starting at the project root.

features.name: scanDocuments
features.signature: void scanDocuments(java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String scanDir) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Scans within a project directory using either a relative start directory or a glob:/regex: matcher. Initializes function tool configuration before scanning, then applies include matching and exclusions while traversing modules and parent files.

examples.description: Scan only Java files anywhere under the project directory using a glob matcher.

features.name: Reviewer SPI (ServiceLoader)
features.signature: java.lang.String perform(java.io.File projectDir, java.io.File file) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Pluggable SPI for file-type specific scanners that extract embedded "@guidance:" directives and produce normalized prompt fragments. Implementations are discovered via java.util.ServiceLoader and mapped by supported file extensions.

examples.description: Discover installed Reviewer implementations using ServiceLoader.

features.name: CLI entry point
features.signature: static void main(java.lang.String[] args) throws java.io.IOException, org.apache.commons.cli.ParseException
features.description: Command-line interface that loads configuration (gw.properties), parses options (projectDir, model, instructions, guidance/act, excludes, threads, input logging, acts directory), resolves scan paths/patterns, and runs scans. Supports default scanDir configuration and interactive input when options are provided without values.

examples.description: Invoke the CLI programmatically to scan Markdown files under the current directory and enable input logging.

customizations.name: Reviewer
customizations.description: Extension point for adding support for additional file formats or custom guidance extraction rules. Implement org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer and register it via Java ServiceLoader so GuidanceProcessor can discover it and map it by file extension.

examples.description: Define a custom Reviewer (e.g., for AsciiDoc) and register it under META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer.

customizations.name: Act templates (TOML) and inheritance
customizations.description: Customize prompt orchestration by creating TOML-based acts loaded from classpath (/acts) and/or a user directory/URL (gw.acts). Acts support basedOn inheritance, prologue/epilogue lists, instructions, inputs templates (String.format), and gw.* runtime options such as threads/excludes/nonRecursive, plus forwarding of other keys into configuration.

examples.description: Configure Act mode to load external acts and execute the built-in help act with a custom prompt.

customizations.name: Function tools
customizations.description: Function tools can be applied to the underlying GenAIProvider via FunctionToolsLoader, enabling tool-augmented behaviors during prompt execution (e.g., Act discovery and inspection).

examples.description: Apply registered function tools to a provider before performing requests.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer
studs.description: SPI for file reviewers that extract "@guidance:" directives and produce formatted prompt fragments for Ghostwriter processing. Implementations declare supported file extensions and are discovered via ServiceLoader.

examples.description: Core SPI surface used by GuidanceProcessor to support multiple file formats.

examples.name: CLI scan with glob include and excludes
examples.description: Scan Java files under the current directory, exclude build and VCS folders, run with a configured GenAI provider/model, and log composed prompt inputs.

examples.name: Act mode execution
examples.command: java -jar gw.jar "glob:." -d . -m "OpenAI:gpt-5.1" --act "help How do I create an act?"
examples.description: Run Ghostwriter in Act mode using the built-in help act and a custom prompt, scanning the current project directory.
